page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor, running at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. The processor includes 36 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific vector extensions like AVX or AMX. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 7395.26 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 212.41 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25974.0 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU result. The server excels in database operations, showing top-tier performance in Redis SET operations at 144.9 million operations per second and Passmark database tests. Static web serving throughput is also in the top tier at 116.84 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is average, and text generation for small LLM models is weak, though text generation for medium and large models falls into the top tier and strong categories respectively.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, this instance is optimized for high-throughput network and EBS workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides high resource density and strong multi-core performance per unit of power and cost compared to traditional x86 architectures, though the lack of local storage requires external EBS volume provisioning. This server supports multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large LLM text generation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but features a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, and database operations, including 144.9 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to weak, the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded capabilities, software compilation speeds, and static web serving throughput. This profile makes the c8gn.16xlarge applicable for network-heavy applications, multi-threaded compute tasks, database hosting, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, 200 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and two hundred gigabits per second baseline network throughput.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation.
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput networking.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core database performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Supports multi-threaded compute, software compilation, and static web serving.
